title: Ethereal Silky Ethiopian Rice Gored Gored
description: A cultured, silky, lazy-sunday Ethiopian dish that blends fragrant jasmine rice with a warm berbere-infused temper, perfect for a quick bento-box lunch.
instructions: Rinse jasmine rice under cold water until clear.
In a pot combine rinsed rice, water, and a pinch of salt; bring to a boil, then reduce heat, cover, and simmer until rice is tender, about 15 minutes.
Heat ghee in a small skillet over medium heat until fragrant.
Add minced garlic, ginger, and diced onion to the skillet, sautéing until the onion softens, about 3 minutes.
Stir in berbere spice and cook for another minute, letting the spices bloom.
Remove skillet from heat and fold in the cooked rice, mixing gently until the rice is evenly coated with the spice mixture.
Sprinkle chopped cilantro and a splash of lemon juice over the rice, tossing lightly to combine.
Divide the silky rice into bento boxes, serve warm or at room temperature.
